feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Is it true that the releases of films on blu ray are graded to be viewed in a di…
entry.content: Is it true that the releases of films on blu ray are graded to be viewed in a different brightness setting than the theatrical film releases? Thus making calibrating your home cinema to the theatrical standard of 48 cd/m2 to be a futile effort?

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Metal Gear Solid
entry.content: I just realized that Metal Gear Solid 1 and 2 are actually an adaptations of the song Tarkus. In this song Tarkus is taken out by Aquatarkus, which is like an amphibious version of Tarkus and he's able to destroy regular Tarkus because he can go in the water just like how Metal Gear RAY is better than Rex because it can go underwater, too.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: resdent evil 9
entry.content: so when leon confronts the white hair guy cuz he has grace he shoots at him a couple of times but the white suit guy dodged the bullets similar to how the agents do in the matrix movies im just wondering why he is able to do that it seems a bit unrealistic to be able to dodge bullets because of a chemical enhancer im sorry but i don't think its possible for a human body to move faster than bullets at extremely close range

entry.title: /b/ Random: Constant feeling of being wathced, Advice needed
entry.content: For the past year, since I've lived in my own house, with just me and my boyfriend, when I'm home alone, I constantly feel like i'm being watched. I'm 19 and have a lot of interpersonal trauma because I was the "Weird Kid" in highschool, people always treated me like I was weird and gross, and some people I didn't even know made a rumor that I raped someone I didn't even know, this was my freshman year, and all throughout highschool, I received constant death threats. I have since graduated and

entry.title: /g/ Technology: /hsg/ - Home Server General
entry.content: Step rack edition previous: >>109030390 READ THE (temp)WIKI! & help by contributing: https://igwiki.lyci.de/wiki/Home_se rver /hsg/ is about learning and expanding your horizons. Know all about NAS? Learn virtualization. Spun up some VMs? Learn about networking by standing up a OPNsense/PFsense box and configuring some VLANs. There's always more to learn and chances to grow. Think you’re god-tier already? Setup OpenStack and report back. >What software should I run? Install Gentoo. Or whatever f

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Professional Engineer License
entry.content: Has anybody ever pursued a Professional Engineer license remotely? I live outside of the U.S. (I'm in the military) and am close to finishing my computer engineering degree. Trying to figure out how I could get a PE to oversee me as I work on a side project so I could get some credibility when I apply to take the PE exam in a few years.

entry.title: /o/ Auto: The era of the small car is over:
entry.content: Behold the new Mercedes VLE 8000 lbs all electric - murky if they will actually make a gas-powered one, but I would assume so eventually, though whether that comes to America is iffy 210-215 inches long (short v long wheelbase) for comparison kia telluride is 200 inches long cadillac escalade is 225 inches long interior is screentastic reviews say the interior is nice but it isn't much fun to drive, and the 2wd lower-power trim model is underpowered for such a heavy (8000 lb!) car. By comparison

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: "who stole the cookie from the cookie jar?" this is one of the biggest lies in m…
entry.content: "who stole the cookie from the cookie jar?" this is one of the biggest lies in music history. the cookie jar is there specifically for people to take cookies as they please. you make a bunch of cookies, you stuff the jar full of them, and you put it on the counter for everybody to access at any time. you don't have to ask to have a cookie. I wouldn't expect YOU, anon, to understand the complexities of my thinking here. Your fortune: Good Luck

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Did your country have their own style of club music back in the 90's? For instan…
entry.content: Did your country have their own style of club music back in the 90's? For instance, in Spain we had makina which was a very fast type of techno. It somehow went mainstream and even reached the UK scene. I know in the Netherlands they started the whole gabber thing with Thunderdome. Italy had this italodisco with Gigi D'Agostino and others. In the US I guess they had the acid house scene. In the UK garage and jungle. I just think it was cool how back then every country had their own scenes and ca

entry.title: /g/ Technology: We must not allow the prohibition of AI-based intelligence measurement.
entry.content: Those who advocate for banning AI-based intelligence testing are powerful figures who intend to steal someone else's work by simply changing the name. If AI were to test intelligence, it could immediately detect fakes, but if intelligence testing is banned, it would be unable to detect them, and the AI would mistakenly identify thieves and fraudsters as superiors.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Drug Use In The Music Industry
entry.content: There's a big misconception in the music community that's been peddled by normies both on social media and in real life regarding drugs in the music community. Most of them seem to be of the opinion that musicians do drugs to "inspire" them to create ground breaking and era defining art/music, but the fact of the matter is, the majority of musicians do drugs to wind down, combat stage fright, to help them focus, and due to the rigorous schedule that comes with being a famous musician. The majori

entry.title: /g/ Technology: what is the technological problem with youtube being unable to recommend new videos?
entry.content: why is that such an impossibility for it not to just reshuffle the existing videos? why must my homepage be refreshed 7+ times just for *one* new video to pop up? Is this some kind of unsolved problem in computer science or what? i remember back in the day youtube felt like an endless stream of new videos. What the fuck happened?
